title: fix races in the HTTP attach API
body:
This is very similar to commit 3280da0500, we cannot check the state then unlock to then lock again and do the action. Everything must happen under one lock. To fix this move the code into the HTTPAttach function in libpod. Th elocking here is a bit weird because attach blocks for the lifetime of attach which can be very long so we must unlock before performing the attach.

Fixes #23757

```release-note
Fixed a race condition in the remote HTTP attach API that may cause it to fail
```

It is still racy but it doesn't error anymore
```
# podman-remote [options] start -a ctr-with-oom-score
           0
           # podman-remote [options] start -a ctr-with-oom-score

           [FAILED] Expected
               <string>: 
           to equal
               <string>: 0
           In [It] at: /var/tmp/go/src/github.com[/containers/podman/test/e2e/run_test.go:738](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/2cbb053819e815090e5cb0f86a1a4d68fe6abd19/test/e2e/run_test.go#L738) @ 08/27/24 05:15:08.325
```
https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6557228600131584/html/int-remote-fedora-40-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-run-podman-run-limits-test--1

Ok very easy to reproduce with this patch
```diff
diff --git a/cmd/podman/containers/cleanup.go b/cmd/podman/containers/cleanup.go
index db15c6d834..42140fb713 100644
--- a/cmd/podman/containers/cleanup.go
+++ b/cmd/podman/containers/cleanup.go
@@ -3,6 +3,7 @@ package containers
 import (
        "errors"
        "fmt"
+       "time"
 
        "github.com/containers/common/pkg/completion"
        "github.com/containers/podman/v5/cmd/podman/common"
@@ -71,6 +72,7 @@ func cleanup(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
                        return errors.New("--exec and --rmi cannot be set together")
                }
        }
+       time.Sleep(5 * time.Second)
 
        responses, err := registry.ContainerEngine().ContainerCleanup(registry.GetContext(), args, cleanupOptions)
        if err != nil {
diff --git a/pkg/domain/infra/tunnel/containers.go b/pkg/domain/infra/tunnel/containers.go
index 6e8c6fea44..2e4b53f652 100644
--- a/pkg/domain/infra/tunnel/containers.go
+++ b/pkg/domain/infra/tunnel/containers.go
@@ -677,6 +677,7 @@ func startAndAttach(ic *ContainerEngine, name string, detachKeys *string, sigPro
                if dk := detachKeys; dk != nil {
                        startOptions.WithDetachKeys(*dk)
                }
+               time.Sleep(10 * time.Second)
                if err := containers.Start(ic.ClientCtx, name, startOptions); err != nil {
                        return err
                }
```
```
$ bin/podman-remote create --name test quay.io/libpod/testimage:20240123 echo hi
$ bin/podman-remote start -a test && echo second && bin/podman-remote start -a test
hi
second
```


What happens is that we init + attach the second time before the podman container cleanup runs but then as attach is unlocked the cleanup commands runs now ending the attach session again before the remote client did the start command.
Because attach and start are two different endpoint there is no way to do both at once.

So to fix this we either add a new API to do both at once or figure out a way to make the cleanup process behave correctly and not cleanup after the ctr was init again..., given the API works like docker we should do the later I think because other API callers will not take advantage of it.

cc @mheon 

And @edsantiago this is likely the cause for https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/23754 as well

Making cleanup (or, at least, cleanup as it is invoked automatically) only work on Stopped containers sounds like the right call. If the container is in any state but Stopped by the time the cleanup process gets to it someone else was there first.

> Making cleanup (or, at least, cleanup as it is invoked automatically) only work on Stopped containers sounds like the right call. If the container is in any state but Stopped by the time the cleanup process gets to it someone else was there first.

Sounds good to me, this should easy to implement as well.

/hold cancel
Verified locally with my sleep patch above the new version seems to work there correctly
@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL, this is fixing active flakes on main
LGTM. I cherrypicked (yesterday) on #23275. That does not run `int` tests, but anyhow, I've seen no new harm from this.
